title: Capacity-aware SLO relaxation with regional soft-eviction safeguard
preview: Context: We saw repeated cascade evictions during multi-region capacity spikes where strict SLO enforcement triggered aggressive circuit breakers, causing higher tail latency and customer throttles (SUP-9812). This PR introduces a capacity-aware, timebound SLO relaxation layer plus a regional soft-eviction safeguard...
text: Context: We saw repeated cascade evictions during multi-region capacity spikes where strict SLO enforcement triggered aggressive circuit breakers, causing higher tail latency and customer throttles (SUP-9812). This PR introduces a capacity-aware, timebound SLO relaxation layer plus a regional soft-eviction safeguard that stages evictions to avoid synchronized mass-drains. What changed: 1) control/allocator: add 'capacity-aware slo_relaxation' policy that dynamically relaxes non-critical SLOs by a configurable percentage when sustained node-utilization exceeds a threshold for a hold window; 2) routing/eviction: introduce 'soft-eviction' state where instances are marked for probabilistic eviction over a decay window instead of immediate hard drain, enabling load to bleed off while respecting per-tenant priority buckets; 3) telemetry: new metrics (slo_relaxation.active, soft_eviction.pen...

title: Introduce transient request coalescer and SLO deceleration gate for burst resilience
preview: Motivation: Short-lived client storms (retries/coordination spikes) can create sharp token-latency SLO violations because many identical or near-identical requests simultaneously hit the fastpath causing KV pressure and circuit flips. This PR introduces two cooperative mitigations: 1) a transient request coalescer a...
text:
Motivation: Short-lived client storms (retries/coordination spikes) can create sharp token-latency SLO violations because many identical or near-identical requests simultaneously hit the fastpath causing KV pressure and circuit flips. This PR introduces two cooperative mitigations: 1) a transient request coalescer at the front of the serving runtime that deduplicates identical requests arriving within a short configurable window (default 25ms) and coalesces them into a single upstream evaluation, and 2) an SLO deceleration gate which, instead of abruptly rejecting when SLO pressure is detected, reduces admission gradually via a short, measurable backoff window (configurable hysteresis) so clients see increased latency rather than immediate hard failures. Both features are behind a runtime feature flag and ship with tracing/metrics and a rollout plan.

What this PR contains:
- Transien...

title: Add regression blame and diff-summary CI check for prompt-set evaluations
preview: Motivation: CI currently reports failing prompt-set metrics but gives limited context to quickly identify the source of a regression. This PR introduces a two-part CI hook: (1) regression-blame which correlates failing prompt cohorts to recent changes in prompt definitions, eval configs, and model pinning; (2) diff-...
text: Motivation: CI currently reports failing prompt-set metrics but gives limited context to quickly identify the source of a regression. This PR introduces a two-part CI hook: (1) regression-blame which correlates failing prompt cohorts to recent changes in prompt definitions, eval configs, and model pinning; (2) diff-summary which renders a compact human-readable summary of model-output changes for affected prompts. The goal is to reduce mean time-to-diagnose for eval regressions and to make gating decisions actionable. Implementation notes: regression-blame walks the prompt-set git history and the eval config commits in the last 30 days to produce candidate commit ranges; it then scores candidates by overlap with changed prompt IDs, changed tokenization metadata, and run-time seed/temperature differences. diff-summary runs a lightweight paired-output comparator that highlights token-le...

title: Gateway: standardize request_id propagation across streaming + non-streaming errors
preview: Context: SDK parity work (ENG-5120) and Support/Oncall feedback (INT-1182) highlighted that `request_id` is not reliably present across all failure modes. Specifically: (1) some gateway-generated errors returned `x-request-id` header but omitted `request_id` in JSON bodies, (2) upstream service errors sometimes used...
text:
Context: SDK parity work (ENG-5120) and Support/Oncall feedback (INT-1182) highlighted that `request_id` is not reliably present across all failure modes. Specifically: (1) some gateway-generated errors returned `x-request-id` header but omitted `request_id` in JSON bodies, (2) upstream service errors sometimes used `requestId` or `trace_id` and the gateway passed them through inconsistently, and (3) streaming (SSE) error paths frequently dropped request_id entirely (disconnects, mid-stream 429/5xx, proxy-induced truncations), which made customer triage painful.

This PR standardizes request ID behavior end-to-end: the gateway guarantees a canonical request identifier exists for every request, propagates it to downstream services, and returns it consistently to the caller in both headers and error bodies/events.

What changed (high level):
- Canonicalization: adopt a single canonical ...

title: silence-probe-reconnect-backfill-and-cancel-deadline-enforcement
preview: Motivation: Customers observed client disconnects that left server-side streaming state in a limbo where tool-calls were retried and KV-cache backfills were skipped, causing higher token costs and functional duplication. This PR introduces a 'silence probe' reconciliation path, a reconnect probe that attempts lightw...
text: Motivation: Customers observed client disconnects that left server-side streaming state in a limbo where tool-calls were retried and KV-cache backfills were skipped, causing higher token costs and functional duplication. This PR introduces a 'silence probe' reconciliation path, a reconnect probe that attempts lightweight session health checks on client reconnects, an explicit cancel-deadline enforcement for in-flight tool/function calls, and safer backfill semantics when reconnect resumes. Summary of changes: 1) server: add silence-probe detection that marks streams as 'candidate-orphan' after configurable idle windows and enqueues a lightweight snapshot probe before evicting; 2) reconnect: on new client handshake include reconnect-probe phase which validates KV-cache continuity and replays missing prefix tokens if safe; 3) cancel-deadline: tool invocation subsystem now tracks cancel ...

title: Wire telemetry events for Suggestions funnel + server-side API usage logging
preview: Context: For the Optimize Suggestions beta, we need a closed-loop funnel to understand which recommendations are useful and where users drop off (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click). This PR wires client-side analytics events from the Console Suggestions list + details drawer, and adds server-side logging/m...
text: Context: For the Optimize Suggestions beta, we need a closed-loop funnel to understand which recommendations are useful and where users drop off (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click). This PR wires client-side analytics events from the Console Suggestions list + details drawer, and adds server-side logging/metrics on the /optimize/suggestions API for usage + debugging (without collecting request/response bodies). Scope: (1) UI events: suggestions_impression, suggestion_expand, suggestion_dismiss, suggestion_apply_click; (2) API logs + counters: optimize_suggestions.list requests, result counts, latency buckets, authz denied; (3) shared schema helpers to keep event props consistent across UI and backend. Non-goals: no PII, no raw prompts/responses, no customer-level savings numbers in analytics events (those remain in product surfaces only). Event properties (UI): org_id (hashe...

title: RBAC: add optimize.suggestions.read scope + enforcement for Suggestions API
preview: Context: the new Console Suggestions view calls the Optimize Suggestions API (list + details) and must respect org/project scoping and RBAC. This PR introduces a dedicated permission scope `optimize.suggestions.read` and enforces it server-side on `/v1/optimize/suggestions` and `/v1/optimize/suggestions/{id}`. Imple...
text: Context: the new Console Suggestions view calls the Optimize Suggestions API (list + details) and must respect org/project scoping and RBAC. This PR introduces a dedicated permission scope `optimize.suggestions.read` and enforces it server-side on `/v1/optimize/suggestions` and `/v1/optimize/suggestions/{id}`. Implementation details: (1) adds the scope to the authz policy bundle and wires it into the API gateway middleware, (2) ensures suggestions queries are always constrained to the caller’s org_id + optional project_id (no cross-tenant leakage), (3) returns a consistent 403 payload (`error_code=INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS`, `required_scope=optimize.suggestions.read`) that the Console can map to the RBAC denied empty state, and (4) emits audit log events for both allow/deny decisions with minimal fields (org_id, actor_id, resource_type=optimize_suggestions, action=read, decision, reque...

title: tenant-prefill allocator + econ-sim CLI and quant-profile wizard
preview: Summary: Introduce a tenant-scoped PrefillAllocator that derives conservative prefill budgets, microbatch sizes, and transient burst lanes from tenant profile histograms; add econ-sim CLI that replays exported traces to estimate token-cost, median and tail latencies, and effective tokens-per-dollar for candidate def...
text: Summary: Introduce a tenant-scoped PrefillAllocator that derives conservative prefill budgets, microbatch sizes, and transient burst lanes from tenant profile histograms; add econ-sim CLI that replays exported traces to estimate token-cost, median and tail latencies, and effective tokens-per-dollar for candidate defaults; include an interactive quantization profile wizard that generates three suggested qprofiles (safe, balanced, aggressive) with estimated quality delta and fallback rules. Motivation: Global defaults are brittle across tenant traffic shapes and lead to uneven unit economics. By simulating tenant-level outcomes and producing tenant-aware prefill and quant defaults, operators can reduce cost while containing regression risk. What changed: 1) runtime: pkg/prefill implements PrefillAllocator, tenant policy engine, and conservative safety multiplier; 2) cli: cmd/econ-sim pr...

title: gateway/errors: standardize error_code enum + docs for SDK retry classification
preview: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity initiative (ENG-5126 / ENG-5130), SDKs need a stable way to classify errors as retryable vs terminal without relying on brittle string matching or status code heuristics alone. We currently have a mix of legacy `code` strings (inconsistent casing/names across service...
text:
Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity initiative (ENG-5126 / ENG-5130), SDKs need a stable way to classify errors as retryable vs terminal without relying on brittle string matching or status code heuristics alone. We currently have a mix of legacy `code` strings (inconsistent casing/names across services), missing codes in a few gateway paths, and doc drift. This PR introduces a single, documented `error_code` enum intended for SDK consumption and cleans up gateway mappings so the field is present consistently across JSON errors (including OpenAI-compat routes via the shared formatter).

What this PR does: (1) Defines a canonical `error_code` enum in the gateway error schema and updates the error formatter to always emit `error_code`, `message`, `type`, `request_id`, `status_code`, and `retryable`. (2) Adds mapping logic from downstream service error payloads and legacy f...

title: Add custodian provenance index and region-pin assertor for KMS-backed RBAC v2
preview: Context: For customer audits and regulatory evidence we need deterministic provenance for RBAC v2 decisions and stronger enforcement that prevents CMKs from being used outside their declared geozone. This PR adds two pieces: (1) a lightweight Policy Provenance Index (PPI) that records the canonical fingerprint of a ...
text: Context: For customer audits and regulatory evidence we need deterministic provenance for RBAC v2 decisions and stronger enforcement that prevents CMKs from being used outside their declared geozone. This PR adds two pieces: (1) a lightweight Policy Provenance Index (PPI) that records the canonical fingerprint of a policy decision, inputs (claims, operator), and the KMS key identifier used to encrypt the envelope; and (2) a Region-Pin Assertor that enforces a mapping between tenant/residency claims and KMS key zone attributes. Motivation: current audit logs are verbose but lack reliable deduplication and a canonical decision identifier; KMS consumption has occasional misrouted keys for tenants with strict data residency requirements. Solution summary: - Introduce a PPI service that runs as an in-process index (persisted to consistency-store) and exposes a hashed decision id (ppi_id) t...

title: Add CI lint for audit event required fields + event_type registry enforcement
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and aiming for 100% coverage for sensitive actions. We’ve now got the schema library + validator and a centralized event_type registry, but we don’t have enforcement at the boundary where producers get added/changed. This PR introduces a CI ga...
text:
Context
We’re standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and aiming for 100% coverage for sensitive actions. We’ve now got the schema library + validator and a centralized event_type registry, but we don’t have enforcement at the boundary where producers get added/changed. This PR introduces a CI gate that prevents:
- emitting an unregistered event_type
- emitting an event missing required fields (per schema v1 + sensitive-action taxonomy requirements)
- re-introducing previously fixed omissions during refactors

What’s in this PR
- Adds a new repo tool: tools/auditlint
 - Parses audit event emission callsites (current patterns: audit.Emit(...) and audit.NewEvent(...).Emit(...))
 - Validates event_type against the centralized registry introduced in pr-21991
 - Validates required fields for the event_type against the v1 field rules (schema + taxonomy requirements)
 - E...

title: api-gateway: consistently emit Retry-After on 429 responses + align SDK error fields
preview: Context: As part of the SDK parity initiative (Python/TS/Go), we need the API gateway to reliably communicate backpressure so clients can respect server guidance and avoid retry storms. Today, 429 responses are inconsistent: some routes include Retry-After, others do not; some error bodies omit fields that SDKs use ...
text: Context: As part of the SDK parity initiative (Python/TS/Go), we need the API gateway to reliably communicate backpressure so clients can respect server guidance and avoid retry storms. Today, 429 responses are inconsistent: some routes include Retry-After, others do not; some error bodies omit fields that SDKs use for typed error classification (code/request_id). This PR makes Retry-After emission deterministic for all 429s generated by the gateway rate limiter and harmonizes the JSON error shape consumed by the SDKs. Changes: (1) Always attach a Retry-After header on gateway-generated 429 responses. The value is computed from the limiter reset/next-allowed time and is emitted as delta-seconds (integer, min 1s). (2) Ensure error responses include consistent top-level and nested fields: status, error.code, error.message, error.type, request_id. (3) Add tests covering Retry-After prese...

title: Audit events for retention + data residency configuration changes (before/after summaries)
preview:
## Summary
This PR instruments the control plane settings surfaces for (a) retention policy changes and (b) data residency / region restrictions changes to emit canonical audit events (schema v1).

The main goal is to close the gap for “sensitive actions” around data handling controls so that Hosted/Dedicated/Privat...
text:
## Summary
This PR instruments the control plane settings surfaces for (a) retention policy changes and (b) data residency / region restrictions changes to emit canonical audit events (schema v1).

The main goal is to close the gap for “sensitive actions” around data handling controls so that Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments produce equivalent audit signals and customers can prove who changed retention/residency settings and what changed.

## Context
- Linear: ENG-4839 (data residency + retention audit events)
- Related: schema v1 + required fields (ENG-4821), redaction rules (ENG-4832), correlation IDs (ENG-4830)
- Customer pressure: SUP-18542 (security review questions on retention/residency auditability)

## What changed
### New/updated audit event types (v1)
Adds audit emission for the following configuration mutations:
- `data.retention.updated`
- `data.residency.updated`

Bo...

title: Add Security FAQ / RFP snippets to Internal Admin (approved-language library + API)
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-class “Security Snippets” support to Internal Admin so we can centrally manage approved FAQ/RFP text blocks (and their versions) that Sales/SE tooling consumes. This is part of the Enterprise Security Enablement Kit refresh: we’ve had repeated drift between Drive/Confluence snippets, HubS...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-class “Security Snippets” support to Internal Admin so we can centrally manage approved FAQ/RFP text blocks (and their versions) that Sales/SE tooling consumes. This is part of the Enterprise Security Enablement Kit refresh: we’ve had repeated drift between Drive/Confluence snippets, HubSpot templates, and ad-hoc copy/paste. The goal is a single source of truth with clear ownership, review metadata, and safe distribution semantics (no restricted evidence in snippets). Motivation / context: Enterprise security reviews are gating Dedicated/Private deals; field teams need fast, consistent answers without over-claiming. Today, approved language lives across Confluence + Drive and gets copied into various tools. This introduces: (1) a canonical snippet store; (2) an API for downstream consumption; (3) RBAC + audit log events for changes; (4) explicit versioning ...

title: Add integration tests: sensitive actions emit v1 audit events
preview: Context: As part of the audit logging standardization work, we need automated verification that every “sensitive action” emits a canonical v1 audit event (and that the payload validates) across Hosted and Private. This PR adds end-to-end integration tests that execute representative sensitive actions and assert that...
text:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization work, we need automated verification that every “sensitive action” emits a canonical v1 audit event (and that the payload validates) across Hosted and Private. This PR adds end-to-end integration tests that execute representative sensitive actions and assert that: (1) an audit event is produced, (2) event_type matches the taxonomy registry, (3) required fields are present and schema_version is set, and (4) correlation fields (request_id/trace_id/admin_action_id) are populated and consistent. Motivation: We have active customer escalations where coverage is hard to prove quickly (SUP-18421, SUP-18477). These tests are intended to become part of our “100% coverage” gate and to provide fast signal on regressions when new admin flows are added.

What’s included: Test harness: A new audit-event test helper that polls the audit ingest te...

title: Usage insights: async CSV export endpoint + signed downloads + audit events
preview: Summary: Adds server-side CSV export for in-console usage insights (route/model breakdown) as an async job with signed downloads and audit logging. This is intended for the Usage/Cost Insights beta flow: user triggers export from the console, export is generated in the background, and the UI polls for completion bef...
text:
Summary: Adds server-side CSV export for in-console usage insights (route/model breakdown) as an async job with signed downloads and audit logging. This is intended for the Usage/Cost Insights beta flow: user triggers export from the console, export is generated in the background, and the UI polls for completion before presenting a signed download link.

Motivation: Customers and internal finance workflows need shareable/exportable reports that match billing totals. Doing this synchronously from the query endpoints was timing out for 7d/30d ranges and made it hard to enforce consistent limits and auditability.

What’s included: (1) New Export API (v1): POST /v1/usage_insights/exports creates an export job; GET /v1/usage_insights/exports/{export_id} returns job status + metadata; POST /v1/usage_insights/exports/{export_id}/download issues a short-lived signed URL for the CSV object. (2...

title: RBAC gating for cost views + CSV export; add audit log events
preview:
Summary: Enforces RBAC for Usage Insights cost views and export flows, adds a workspace-level setting to hide cost data, and emits audit log events for export generation/download and share-link access. This is a prerequisite for enabling the Usage Insights beta for enterprise workspaces with stricter governance.

Co...
text:
Summary: Enforces RBAC for Usage Insights cost views and export flows, adds a workspace-level setting to hide cost data, and emits audit log events for export generation/download and share-link access. This is a prerequisite for enabling the Usage Insights beta for enterprise workspaces with stricter governance.

Context / motivation: Usage Insights introduces cost/usage breakdowns by route+model and a CSV export path. Security review flagged two gaps: (1) existing "usage" permissions were sufficient to view token counts but not appropriate for cost visibility, and (2) export/download actions were not consistently audited. We also need a workspace admin control to disable cost visibility in restricted workspaces while still allowing non-cost usage metrics.

What changed:
- RBAC: Introduced a dedicated permission gate for cost visibility and export actions.
 - New permission: cost.read...

title: Add Usage Insights freshness/quality dashboard + alerts (lag + reconciliation drift)
preview: Motivation: Usage Insights is going into beta with interactive console queries backed by hourly/day partitions and reconciliation checks against billing. We need a standard dashboard + alert bundle in observability-pack so SRE/on-call can quickly answer: (1) is data fresh, (2) are we dropping/late-ingesting events b...
text:
Motivation: Usage Insights is going into beta with interactive console queries backed by hourly/day partitions and reconciliation checks against billing. We need a standard dashboard + alert bundle in observability-pack so SRE/on-call can quickly answer: (1) is data fresh, (2) are we dropping/late-ingesting events by route/model, (3) are we drifting vs billing totals beyond tolerance, and (4) are query endpoints timing out for common ranges.

This PR adds a Grafana dashboard and alert rule templates for freshness lag, late event rate, reconciliation drift, and query p95 latency. The dashboard is designed to be imported into customer-facing/internal stacks with minimal per-cluster edits (templated variables for env/region/deployment_mode/workspace_id).

What’s included: 1) Dashboard: “Usage Insights — Data Freshness & Quality” (Grafana JSON) Panels: Aggregation lag (p50/p95) by dataset...

title: Audit logs for shadow experiment + shadow policy changes
preview: Summary: Adds audit log entries for shadow experiment lifecycle actions (create/update/archive) and for route-attached shadow policy updates. This is part of Shadow Traffic Quality Metrics beta readiness: we need a clear who/what/when trail for experiment changes and policy toggles, especially for Dedicated/Private ...
text:
Summary: Adds audit log entries for shadow experiment lifecycle actions (create/update/archive) and for route-attached shadow policy updates. This is part of Shadow Traffic Quality Metrics beta readiness: we need a clear who/what/when trail for experiment changes and policy toggles, especially for Dedicated/Private tenants with compliance requirements.

Context / Motivation: Shadow traffic is explicitly “fail-open” and non-user-impacting, but misconfiguration can still create meaningful cost/retention risk (sampling rate, targets, redaction flags). During dogfood and early customer discussions, we’ve been asked to provide auditable records for: (1) who created/changed an experiment, (2) who attached/detached shadow policies to routes/rollouts, and (3) what fields changed. This PR extends the existing AuditLog service to capture the relevant actions and adds minimal schema support for ...

title: Console API: shadow experiments CRUD + aggregates endpoints (RBAC, pagination)
preview: Summary: Implements Console backend endpoints to create/update/query Shadow Experiments and fetch quality/latency/cost aggregates for Console drilldowns. This is the first public-facing server API for shadow experiments; UI work will follow behind a feature flag. Motivation: Beta customers requested a safe way to at...
text: Summary: Implements Console backend endpoints to create/update/query Shadow Experiments and fetch quality/latency/cost aggregates for Console drilldowns. This is the first public-facing server API for shadow experiments; UI work will follow behind a feature flag. Motivation: Beta customers requested a safe way to attach quality signals to rollouts without impacting user traffic; this PR unblocks dogfood and Dedicated beta by providing stable CRUD + query primitives with strict RBAC and cardinality-safe filters. API surface (v1, behind feature flag `shadow_experiments_api`): POST /v1/shadow-experiments (create), GET /v1/shadow-experiments (list w/ cursor pagination), GET /v1/shadow-experiments/:id (details), PATCH /v1/shadow-experiments/:id (mutate mutable fields only), POST /v1/shadow-experiments/:id:archive (soft-archive), GET /v1/shadow-experiments/:id/aggregates (time-windowed metr...

title: Console: marketplace onboarding entrypoint + improved entitlement error handling
preview: Summary: Adds a marketplace-specific entrypoint to the Console onboarding flow (AWS/GCP) with clearer states for entitlement linking and improved error handling/retry guidance. Motivation: Marketplace buyers are currently landing in the generic signup flow and getting stuck on entitlement propagation and “already ha...
text: Summary: Adds a marketplace-specific entrypoint to the Console onboarding flow (AWS/GCP) with clearer states for entitlement linking and improved error handling/retry guidance. Motivation: Marketplace buyers are currently landing in the generic signup flow and getting stuck on entitlement propagation and “already has org” edge cases; this PR adds a dedicated path and maps entitlement verification errors to actionable UX. Scope: (1) New /onboarding/marketplace route and “Continue from AWS/GCP Marketplace” CTA on the signup screen behind feature flag console.marketplaceOnboardingEntrypoint. (2) Marketplace-specific stepper screens: Connect subscription → Link or create Redwood org → Create first API key → Next steps. (3) Error mapping for entitlement verification endpoint responses (from PR #19875) with user-facing copy, retry timers, and support handoff. (4) Edge-case handling: existin...

title: Shadow traffic: retention defaults + prompt/response redaction pipeline
preview: Context: Shadow Traffic generates additional artifacts (request/response pairs + scoring payload references). We need strict data minimization by default and deterministic redaction so we can store enough to compute quality deltas while meeting Dedicated/Private constraints. This PR implements (1) retention defaults...
text: Context: Shadow Traffic generates additional artifacts (request/response pairs + scoring payload references). We need strict data minimization by default and deterministic redaction so we can store enough to compute quality deltas while meeting Dedicated/Private constraints. This PR implements (1) retention defaults by deployment mode, (2) a redaction/transform pipeline for shadow artifacts, and (3) configuration toggles + audit-friendly metadata to support regulated customers. Summary of changes: - Adds `shadow_artifact_retention` config with safe defaults (Hosted: 24h; Dedicated: 7d; Private: 0h unless explicitly configured) and per-tenant overrides with validation. - Introduces `ShadowArtifactRedactor` (server-side) that transforms prompts/inputs and outputs before persistence: structured allowlist, truncation, hashing of identifiers, and regex-based PII scrubbing. - Adds storage l...

title: Add schema_version tagging, JSON Schema validation, and legacy event normalization to v1
preview:
## Summary
This PR updates the audit-log-exporter to:
- Tag all outgoing events with `schema_version` (default: `v1.0`).
- Validate outgoing payloads against the canonical Audit Log Schema v1 (JSON Schema) and emit exporter-side metrics when validation fails.
- Normalize legacy/heterogeneous producer payloads into t...
text:
## Summary
This PR updates the audit-log-exporter to:
- Tag all outgoing events with `schema_version` (default: `v1.0`).
- Validate outgoing payloads against the canonical Audit Log Schema v1 (JSON Schema) and emit exporter-side metrics when validation fails.
- Normalize legacy/heterogeneous producer payloads into the v1 canonical shape to eliminate field drift across event types.

This is the first step to making SIEM exports consistent across Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments and to support SOC2/ISO evidence expectations (stable schema + documented backcompat behavior).

## Motivation
We have multiple event producers emitting “almost the same” semantics but with different field names and shapes. Customers exporting to Splunk/Sentinel/Datadog see inconsistent keys across event types (SUP-18477). Additionally, exporter retries can cause duplicates (SUP-18510); while idempotency is ...

title: Policy engine: emit v1 audit events for routing policy + fallback updates (diff + cardinality checks)
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing audit logging coverage for sensitive actions across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Routing policy changes (incl. fallback rules) are security-relevant because they can alter what models serve which requests, potentially impacting data handling boundaries and reliability controls.

This PR inst...
text:
Context
We’re standardizing audit logging coverage for sensitive actions across Hosted/Dedicated/Private. Routing policy changes (incl. fallback rules) are security-relevant because they can alter what models serve which requests, potentially impacting data handling boundaries and reliability controls.

This PR instruments the policy engine to emit schema v1 audit events for routing policy CRUD + publish operations, with:
- A bounded diff generator (before/after) to support investigations
- Cardinality guards to prevent high-cardinality payloads from polluting downstream SIEMs
- Consistent actor/target semantics + correlation IDs per the v1 standard

What’s included
- Audit events emitted for:
 - Routing policy create/update/delete
 - Fallback configuration update (primary/fallback model sets, failure-mode rules)
 - Allow/deny list changes for model routing
 - Policy publish/activate ...

title: Shadow traffic: telemetry events + ingestion hooks (v1)
preview: Context: Shadow traffic routing (ENG-5402) mirrors a sampled subset of prod requests to one or more shadow targets. To compute quality deltas (and to debug routing/scoring lag) we need a low-cardinality, privacy-aware set of telemetry events that (a) links the control and shadow inference outcomes, (b) can be ingest...
text:
Context: Shadow traffic routing (ENG-5402) mirrors a sampled subset of prod requests to one or more shadow targets. To compute quality deltas (and to debug routing/scoring lag) we need a low-cardinality, privacy-aware set of telemetry events that (a) links the control and shadow inference outcomes, (b) can be ingested reliably in Hosted/Dedicated/Private, and (c) provides enough dimensions to slice by route/model/region without exploding metrics cardinality.

This PR introduces shadow traffic telemetry events and wires them into the ingestion pipeline (Kafka -> ingest -> warehouse tables) with initial dashboard stubs for internal dogfood.

What’s included: (1) New event schemas: shadow_pair_created (emitted when the mirror is scheduled), shadow_inference_completed (shadow target returned or timed out), and shadow_scoring_status (async scoring queued/completed/failed). Each event inclu...

title: Control plane: emit v1 audit events for RBAC CRUD endpoints
preview: Context: We’re standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private with a canonical v1 schema. RBAC is a top-tier sensitive surface (role definitions, role bindings, membership changes), and we’ve had customer reports of missing RBAC change events in Private (SUP-18421). This PR instruments the control plane...
text: Context: We’re standardizing audit logging across Hosted/Dedicated/Private with a canonical v1 schema. RBAC is a top-tier sensitive surface (role definitions, role bindings, membership changes), and we’ve had customer reports of missing RBAC change events in Private (SUP-18421). This PR instruments the control plane RBAC service (and the HTTP layer that fronts it) to emit v1 audit events for RBAC CRUD actions with consistent actor attribution, safe diff summaries, and correlation IDs (request_id/trace_id/admin_action_id) for investigations. What’s included: (1) v1 audit event emission for RBAC endpoints: role.create, role.update, role.delete; role_binding.create/update/delete; membership add/remove; project/tenant-scoped permission bundle updates. (2) Actor identity + impersonation support: actor.type (user|service_account|service), actor.id, actor.email (when available), actor.sessio...

title: Audit events: API key create/rotate/revoke (v1 schema + redaction)
preview: Summary This PR adds canonical v1 audit events for API key lifecycle actions in the control plane: creation, rotation (new key issued for an existing key record), and revocation. The events are emitted for both API and Console-backed workflows and follow the audit schema library introduced in pr-21984 and the event_...
text: Summary This PR adds canonical v1 audit events for API key lifecycle actions in the control plane: creation, rotation (new key issued for an existing key record), and revocation. The events are emitted for both API and Console-backed workflows and follow the audit schema library introduced in pr-21984 and the event_type registry from pr-21991. Motivation API keys are a high-risk credential surface and are explicitly called out as “sensitive actions” for SOC2/ISO-aligned control evidence and customer investigations. Prior to this PR we had partial/legacy logging with inconsistent field names and (in one path) a debug log that could include a key prefix. This PR standardizes the audit semantics and hardens redaction so key material is never persisted to the audit stream. Event types added (registered in audit registry) - credential.api_key.created - credential.api_key.rotated - credenti...

title: Audit logging: register sensitive-action event types and reject unknown event_type values
preview: Summary: Introduces a centralized registry for audit log event_type values (sensitive actions taxonomy) and enforces that all emitted audit events use a registered event_type. Enforcement happens in two places: (1) build-time lint/codegen to prevent new event types from being introduced ad-hoc, and (2) runtime valid...
text:
Summary: Introduces a centralized registry for audit log event_type values (sensitive actions taxonomy) and enforces that all emitted audit events use a registered event_type. Enforcement happens in two places: (1) build-time lint/codegen to prevent new event types from being introduced ad-hoc, and (2) runtime validation in the shared audit event emitter to optionally reject (or warn on) unknown types.

Motivation: As we drive to 100% coverage for sensitive actions (ENG-4822), we need consistent semantics across services and deployments (Hosted/Dedicated/Private). In the current state, event_type strings are free-form. This makes SIEM normalization brittle and makes coverage dashboards noisy (slight spelling variants create “new” types). Centralizing the taxonomy also reduces cardinality risk and improves backcompat guarantees.

Design: - Source-of-truth registry lives in repo at audi...

title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels
preview:
Title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels

Context
During recent gamedays + the SUP-18421 escalation, the biggest time sink was not executing rollback itself, but getting responders to the right place quickly (Console rollback view + the canonical runbook) while c...
text:
Title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels

Context
During recent gamedays + the SUP-18421 escalation, the biggest time sink was not executing rollback itself, but getting responders to the right place quickly (Console rollback view + the canonical runbook) while context switching across tabs. This PR adds a tiny “fast path” in incident-bot: a slash command that posts the correct links and a short checklist directly into the incident channel.

What’s included
- New Slack slash command: `/rollback` (alias `/rw-rollback`).
- Posts a message with:
 - Link to Console Rollback UI (Optimization Config history view with the rollback modal entry point).
 - Link to the runbook: “Rollback Optimization Config”.
 - A reminder of the expected decision criteria (p95/p99 regression + 5xx) and the post-rollback verification checklist.
- Optional arg...

title: Optimize quality gates: add override flow w/ expiration + audit events (admin-only)
preview:
## Summary
Implements the time-bound override workflow for Optimize quality gate failures. This enables an authorized approver to temporarily override a failed gate (or a blocked apply) with an explicit reason, expiration, and an auditable trail.

Key properties:
- Overrides are **time-bound** (required `expires_at`...
text:
## Summary
Implements the time-bound override workflow for Optimize quality gate failures. This enables an authorized approver to temporarily override a failed gate (or a blocked apply) with an explicit reason, expiration, and an auditable trail.

Key properties:
- Overrides are **time-bound** (required `expires_at`) with max TTL enforcement.
- Only users with an **admin override role** may grant/extend/revoke overrides.
- Overrides are **scoped** (route + model + proposed config diff hash) to avoid blanket bypass.
- All override actions emit **audit events** (request/grant/deny/revoke/expire) and are queryable.
- Optimize apply and rollout promotion paths treat an active override as an explicit exception (still logged).

This PR is part of the “Quality guardrails gating for Optimize” project and lands the last missing piece needed for limited beta (see ENG-3927 / PM-482).

## Motivat...

title: Add gate-run artifact links to Console-facing Optimize APIs
preview: Context: Optimize quality gates now produce structured results + artifacts (eval report HTML/JSON, metric breakdowns, prompt-set diff links, rerun provenance). Console needs stable, permissioned references to those artifacts to render the gate results view and the “blocked apply” reason details. This PR extends the ...
text: Context: Optimize quality gates now produce structured results + artifacts (eval report HTML/JSON, metric breakdowns, prompt-set diff links, rerun provenance). Console needs stable, permissioned references to those artifacts to render the gate results view and the “blocked apply” reason details. This PR extends the Console-facing Optimize API responses to include (a) a compact gate result summary for each recommendation/config-diff and (b) artifact references (URLs or ids) that the UI can link to. What changed: Added `gate_summary` and `artifact_refs` fields to the Optimize recommendation payload and to the gate-run detail endpoint; `gate_summary` includes gate_run_id, state, tier (smoke/full), baseline_id, started_at/completed_at, top-level pass/fail reason codes, and a small set of headline deltas (e.g., task_success_delta, rubric_delta) for UI tables; `artifact_refs` includes repor...

title: Block Optimize config apply when quality gate fails + surface failure reasons
preview: Context: Optimize recommendations can change serving behavior (batching / caching / quantization profiles). We already persist gate runs and compute pass/fail, but there were still paths where an operator (or automation) could call the apply endpoint even if the gate was failed or still running. This PR enforces qua...
text:
Context: Optimize recommendations can change serving behavior (batching / caching / quantization profiles). We already persist gate runs and compute pass/fail, but there were still paths where an operator (or automation) could call the apply endpoint even if the gate was failed or still running. This PR enforces quality gating at the final boundary: the ApplyConfig operation.

What this PR does: (1) Adds a mandatory precondition check in OptimizeService ApplyConfig: if the target route/policy requires gating, the request must reference a gate_run_id and that gate run must be in PASSED or OVERRIDDEN state at time of apply. (2) Returns explicit, structured failure reasons (reason_code + human message) and stable identifiers for the UI/automation to link to the gate artifacts. (3) Adds a Console deep-link URL and API field to allow the Console to render “Blocked by quality gate” with one...

title: Console: add rollback trigger endpoint with server-side confirmation checks
preview:
Adds the Console backend endpoint used by the new “Rollback” button for Optimization Config versions. This PR focuses on server-side confirmation/guardrails so the Console UI can remain thin and we avoid dangerous client-only checks.

Key behavior
- New endpoint: POST /internal/console/optimization-config/rollbacks
...
text:
Adds the Console backend endpoint used by the new “Rollback” button for Optimization Config versions. This PR focuses on server-side confirmation/guardrails so the Console UI can remain thin and we avoid dangerous client-only checks.

Key behavior
- New endpoint: POST /internal/console/optimization-config/rollbacks
 - Triggers a rollback for a given scope (tenant + model + region) to a target config_version_id.
 - Returns a rollback_operation_id and the initial operation state.
- Confirmation model
 - Requires a confirmation payload computed server-side and echoed back by the client (type-to-confirm UX can be driven from this).
 - The server recomputes the confirmation on request and rejects if it doesn’t match (prevents stale modal confirmations).
- Guardrails (server-enforced)
 - Blast-radius summary + limits: rejects requests that exceed allowed scope cardinality for Console-trigge...

title: Add Optimization Config change permissions + operator roles (apply/promote/rollback)
preview: Summary: Introduces explicit RBAC permissions for the Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) and adds two opinionated roles used by Console + control plane workflows. This is part of the Safe apply + rollback mechanism project to ensure rollback is both auditable and access-controlled...
text: Summary: Introduces explicit RBAC permissions for the Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) and adds two opinionated roles used by Console + control plane workflows. This is part of the Safe apply + rollback mechanism project to ensure rollback is both auditable and access-controlled. Motivation: Today, optimization config changes are implicitly covered by broad “platform operator” capabilities in some environments, and not consistently modeled in Private deployments. Enterprise customers and Security review require least-privilege roles with clear separation between “can propose” and “can execute rollback in prod.” Changes included: 1) New permissions: optimization_config.read, optimization_config.create_version, optimization_config.validate, optimization_config.diff, optimization_config.apply, optimization_config.promote, optimization_config.rollback...

title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations
preview: Title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations. Context: As part of offline bundle signature verification, the installer now supports a policy-gated break-glass flag (see redwood-private-installer pr-24979). This PR adds cluster-side guardrails so that (1) break-glass cannot be ...
text: Title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations. Context: As part of offline bundle signature verification, the installer now supports a policy-gated break-glass flag (see redwood-private-installer pr-24979). This PR adds cluster-side guardrails so that (1) break-glass cannot be enabled silently, (2) use is attributable and exportable via audit logs, and (3) only approved operators can authorize it. Summary of changes: (A) Adds a Gatekeeper ConstraintTemplate + Constraint enforcing required annotations whenever break-glass is enabled via the Redwood Private install metadata ConfigMap (redwood-private/install-metadata) or Helm values rendered into that ConfigMap. Required annotations: redwood.ai/break-glass-ticket (SUP-/INT-/customer ticket id), redwood.ai/break-glass-reason (freeform, min 20 chars), redwood.ai/break-glass-approved-by (corporate ema...

title: Shadow traffic: deterministic request fingerprint + bucketing v1
preview: Context: Shadow traffic needs consistent comparisons across time windows and across control/shadow targets. The earlier routing mirror MVP used per-request RNG sampling which made deltas noisy and made it hard to correlate control vs shadow outputs for the same logical request. This PR adds a deterministic request f...
text:
Context: Shadow traffic needs consistent comparisons across time windows and across control/shadow targets. The earlier routing mirror MVP used per-request RNG sampling which made deltas noisy and made it hard to correlate control vs shadow outputs for the same logical request. This PR adds a deterministic request fingerprint and a stable bucketing/assignment helper used by the data plane when deciding whether to mirror a request and which shadow target(s) to send it to.

What’s in this PR: 1) Request fingerprinting (fingerprint_v1): Computes a 256-bit fingerprint as HMAC-SHA256(tenant_shadow_secret, canonical_request_bytes). Canonical bytes are derived from a canonical JSON encoding of a strictly limited set of fields: route_id, request_kind (chat/completions/embeddings), model_group (logical), normalized generation params (temperature/top_p/max_tokens/etc), and the request content p...

title: Control plane: add ShadowTrafficPolicy schema + validation + migration scaffolding
preview: Summary: This PR introduces the control-plane schema for ShadowTrafficPolicy and wiring needed to store/validate it in route/rollout configs. This is the first building block for shadow traffic (mirroring sampled requests to one or more shadow targets) and is intentionally scoped to schema/validation + migration sca...
text: Summary: This PR introduces the control-plane schema for ShadowTrafficPolicy and wiring needed to store/validate it in route/rollout configs. This is the first building block for shadow traffic (mirroring sampled requests to one or more shadow targets) and is intentionally scoped to schema/validation + migration scaffolding only (no data-plane mirroring, no telemetry). Motivation: We need a stable, versioned policy object that can be attached to routes and rollout variants, validated at write time, and safely no-oped until the data plane and telemetry paths are enabled. This keeps the upcoming work (ENG-5402/5403/5404) unblocked while making sure we don’t paint ourselves into a corner on defaults, RBAC, and security-sensitive fields. What’s included: (1) New schema objects: ShadowTrafficPolicy v1 and ShadowTargetRef. (2) Validation: sampling is bounded; max shadow targets enforced; ti...

title: Emit structured audit log events for Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) + schema updates
preview: Context: Optimization config changes (batching/caching/quantization profiles) are production-impacting and must be traceable and reviewable. We’ve had gaps reported by enterprise customers (SUP-18544) and internal operators (INT-3390) where rollback actions were not consistently visible in audit exports, and some li...
text: Context: Optimization config changes (batching/caching/quantization profiles) are production-impacting and must be traceable and reviewable. We’ve had gaps reported by enterprise customers (SUP-18544) and internal operators (INT-3390) where rollback actions were not consistently visible in audit exports, and some lifecycle steps were only logged in app logs. This PR standardizes and emits structured audit events for the full Optimization Config lifecycle and updates the audit schema registry accordingly. What’s included: (1) New/standardized event names under the "optimization_config" namespace: optimization_config.version_created, optimization_config.version_validated, optimization_config.apply_requested, optimization_config.rollout_started, optimization_config.rollout_paused, optimization_config.rollout_resumed, optimization_config.rollout_step_completed, optimization_config.rollout...

title: Add rollback endpoint for Optimization Config versions (authz + audit events)
preview: Motivation: We need a safe, auditable way to revert optimization changes (batching/caching/quant profiles) without requiring a manual DB edit or redeploy. This PR adds the initial rollback API endpoint to the Optimization Config Service, including RBAC authorization, audit-log emission, and integration tests that co...
text: Motivation: We need a safe, auditable way to revert optimization changes (batching/caching/quant profiles) without requiring a manual DB edit or redeploy. This PR adds the initial rollback API endpoint to the Optimization Config Service, including RBAC authorization, audit-log emission, and integration tests that codify the expected semantics for callers (Console + internal tooling). API: Adds POST /v1/optimization-configs/rollbacks to request a rollback for a given scope (tenant/model/region) to a target config_version_id (or "previous_promoted" shortcut). The endpoint creates a rollback operation record, performs server-side validation (scope match, version exists/visible, cooldown, and active rollout compatibility), and returns an operation_id that the rollout controller/runtime applier will observe. Authz: Enforces new permission "optimization_config.rollback" and validates caller...

title: Emit audit event on successful offline bundle verification (Redwood Private)
preview: Summary: Add a first-class audit event for offline bundle verification successes and wire an emission path that Private/on-prem deployments can use for verification reporting. This complements existing failure events emitted by the installer/preflight tooling and enables customers to prove (locally, and optionally e...
text:
Summary: Add a first-class audit event for offline bundle verification successes and wire an emission path that Private/on-prem deployments can use for verification reporting. This complements existing failure events emitted by the installer/preflight tooling and enables customers to prove (locally, and optionally exported) that an installation/upgrade was performed from a bundle that verified cleanly.

Context: As part of the on-prem offline artifact signing work, Support/Solutions and customer security teams requested a positive attestation signal in the audit trail. Today we record failures (hash mismatch / signature invalid / unknown key), but there is no canonical success event that includes the bundle identity + verification metadata. This PR adds the event definition to the redwood audit event catalog and provides a helper to emit it from Private installer pathways that call in...

title: Add least-privilege RBAC for release signing service operator
preview:
## Summary
Introduce a dedicated, least-privilege RBAC role for "release signing operators" who need to initiate and monitor offline bundle signing runs for Redwood Private releases.

This PR removes the current ad-hoc guidance of granting broad permissions in the release-tools cluster and replaces it with an explic...
text:
## Summary
Introduce a dedicated, least-privilege RBAC role for "release signing operators" who need to initiate and monitor offline bundle signing runs for Redwood Private releases.

This PR removes the current ad-hoc guidance of granting broad permissions in the release-tools cluster and replaces it with an explicit, reviewable policy bundle:
- New `ClusterRole` + namespace-scoped bindings for `release-signing-operator`
- Separation-of-duties: operator can create/approve signing requests and observe jobs, but cannot read signing key material (Secrets) or mutate trust roots
- Policy tests to prevent accidental wildcard RBAC in this area

Context: offline bundle signing pipeline workstream (ENG-4822) + trust store/key rotation migration (ENG-4825). This is intended to be deployed alongside the signing service rollout in the release-tools cluster.

## Motivation / background
As part of...

title: Expose Optimization Config Versions API (list/get/diff) for Console + tooling
preview: This PR adds the read-only API surface for Optimization Config versioning: list versions (with filters + pagination), fetch version details, and retrieve diffs between versions. These endpoints are required for the Console history/timeline and the upcoming safe-apply workflow, and they are also used by internal auto...
text: This PR adds the read-only API surface for Optimization Config versioning: list versions (with filters + pagination), fetch version details, and retrieve diffs between versions. These endpoints are required for the Console history/timeline and the upcoming safe-apply workflow, and they are also used by internal automation (release tooling / incident response) to quickly answer “what config is active for tenant/model/region?” and “what changed between v12 and v13?”. Scope: hosted + dedicated control-plane APIs (private installer/helm wiring will land separately). Non-goals: create/apply/promote/rollback are not included here; this PR is strictly read-only + diff retrieval and enforces RBAC. API additions: (1) Public (customer/admin) endpoints under /v1/optimize/configs: GET /v1/optimize/configs/{config_id}/versions?scope=...&limit=...&cursor=... returns immutable version metadata sorte...

title: Demo tenant: scope API keys to least privilege + rotation helpers + audit log coverage
preview: Context: SEs and AEs have been sharing a single broadly-permissioned demo API key for convenience. This has caused a few recurring problems: (1) unclear blast radius when a key leaks in a screen share/recording, (2) brittle access when RBAC mappings change (INT-993), (3) hard-to-debug 429s / quota overrides (SUP-412...
text: Context: SEs and AEs have been sharing a single broadly-permissioned demo API key for convenience. This has caused a few recurring problems: (1) unclear blast radius when a key leaks in a screen share/recording, (2) brittle access when RBAC mappings change (INT-993), (3) hard-to-debug 429s / quota overrides (SUP-4127) because traffic sources are indistinguishable, and (4) audit log gaps for key lifecycle events. This PR tightens demo-tenant API key scopes to least privilege, adds rotation helpers for the demo reset flow, and ensures we emit consistent audit-log events for key create/rotate/revoke. Summary of changes (single-tenant + parallel demos friendly): 1) New scoped key model for demo workflows: - Introduce explicit permission bundles for demo use cases: `demo.chat.invoke`, `demo.embeddings.invoke`, `demo.routes.read`, `demo.console.read`, `demo.eval.run` (internal), `demo.traff...

title: Add KMS throttle budget + request backpressure to prevent KMS saturation under spikes
preview: Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope...
text:
Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope ops (e.g. cache cold start + traffic spike) could saturate KMS and extend the incident blast radius.

This PR introduces an explicit "KMS throttle budget" and integrates KMS calls with the existing request admission/backpressure path so we shed/queue work earlier rather than stampeding KMS.

What’s included (high level): (1) Per-process token-bucket budget for KMS operations: A lightweight budget that is decremented on KMS requests and refilled at a configured rate. When depleted, KMS calls are delayed (bounded) or rejected with a typed error depending on caller policy. Th...

title: Add config guardrails for KMS timeout/retry budgets (prod-safe defaults + explicit override)
preview:
Context: As part of the KMS hardening work (ADR-007) and following the recent near-miss retry amplification behavior, we need a hard stop on “retry storm by config.” We currently allow operators to dial up timeouts/retries in config, which in prod can turn a transient KMS degradation into cascading request pileups.
...
text:
Context: As part of the KMS hardening work (ADR-007) and following the recent near-miss retry amplification behavior, we need a hard stop on “retry storm by config.” We currently allow operators to dial up timeouts/retries in config, which in prod can turn a transient KMS degradation into cascading request pileups.

What this PR does: Introduces validation + guardrails for KMS client config so overly aggressive timeout/retry settings cannot be enabled in production without an explicit, time-bound override. This is enforced in two places: (1) control-plane config validation when publishing/rolling out config; (2) service startup validation as a backstop for manually edited configs/private installs.

Guardrails (prod): - kms.timeout_ms must be within [200, 2000]. - kms.max_retries must be within [0, 4]. - kms.max_retry_elapsed_ms must be within [0, 5000]. - computed “worst-case budget” ...

title: Introduce credential-harmonizer and backpressure-shim across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs
preview: Context and motivation: We see two operational issues that cause instability at scale: (1) SDKs often trigger near-simultaneous credential refreshes when TTL windows align, producing spikes and transient 5xx cascades; (2) long-lived streaming consumers lack a compact way to communicate backpressure to the runtime, w...
text:
Context and motivation: We see two operational issues that cause instability at scale: (1) SDKs often trigger near-simultaneous credential refreshes when TTL windows align, producing spikes and transient 5xx cascades; (2) long-lived streaming consumers lack a compact way to communicate backpressure to the runtime, which leads to unbounded client queues and memory pressure in bursty workloads. This PR implements a light-weight, opt-in Credential Harmonizer and a Backpressure Shim across Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs to harmonize refresh signals and expose consumer pressure hints.

What this PR does: - Introduces a Credential Harmonizer protocol and local in-process coordinator in each SDK that: * deduplicates concurrent refresh attempts for the same logical session * jitter-aligns refresh windows across instances to avoid refresh storms * surfaces a small, debuggable trace/span when ...

title: Implement KMS circuit breaker + fallback mode to prevent retry storms during KMS incidents
preview: Context: During recent KMS throttling/timeouts (see SUP-19377), application-side retries amplified load and created a request storm against KMS, worsening tail latency and elevating 5xx. We’ve already standardized timeouts/retries in the KMS wrapper (PR #18421); this change adds circuit breaking + a documented degra...
text:
Context: During recent KMS throttling/timeouts (see SUP-19377), application-side retries amplified load and created a request storm against KMS, worsening tail latency and elevating 5xx. We’ve already standardized timeouts/retries in the KMS wrapper (PR #18421); this change adds circuit breaking + a documented degrade/fallback policy so we fail fast and shed load instead of stampeding KMS.

What this PR does:
- Adds a per-key (key ARN) circuit breaker in the shared KMS client wrapper with CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN states.
- Introduces a fallback mode policy for decrypt/envelope operations:
 - If circuit is OPEN: do not call KMS; attempt to serve from in-memory DEK cache if present (bounded TTL, non-persistent); otherwise fail fast with a mapped KMSUnavailable error (no retries).
 - Encrypt/new key generation paths: fail fast when OPEN (no cache fallback), returning a distinct error class ...

title: Add audit events for image channel/version changes on fleet pools
preview:
## Context
As part of the provision-to-ready automation rollout, we are standardizing golden image channels (candidate -> stable) and pinning pool image versions. During the us-east/A100 staged rollout we had a quarantine spike and had to quickly determine who/what changed image configuration on the affected pool(s)...
text:
## Context
As part of the provision-to-ready automation rollout, we are standardizing golden image channels (candidate -> stable) and pinning pool image versions. During the us-east/A100 staged rollout we had a quarantine spike and had to quickly determine who/what changed image configuration on the affected pool(s). Today those changes are visible via config history in a few places, but they are not captured as first-class audit events.

This PR emits explicit audit events whenever a pool’s image channel or pinned image version changes (including promotions and rollbacks). These events are designed to be consumable by SecOps/compliance tooling and also useful during incident response/postmortems.

## What’s included
- New audit event type: `fleet.pool.image_config_changed`
- Events emitted on:
 - `image_channel` change (e.g., `candidate` -> `stable`)
 - pinned `image_version` change ...

title: Audit log: emit model change events + internal admin query endpoints
preview: Problem: Support/Security need authoritative, queryable audit history for model metadata changes (create/update/promote/deprecate/rollback) to answer enterprise escalations (e.g., “what changed, who did it, when, and in which deployment?”) and to satisfy audit/retention expectations for Private/VPC. Today we emit so...
text:
Problem: Support/Security need authoritative, queryable audit history for model metadata changes (create/update/promote/deprecate/rollback) to answer enterprise escalations (e.g., “what changed, who did it, when, and in which deployment?”) and to satisfy audit/retention expectations for Private/VPC. Today we emit some control-plane events for changelog generation, but they’re not shaped as audit events, not consistently retained, and there’s no internal support surface to query by model/version/workspace.

This PR adds (1) first-class audit log events for model metadata and promotion lifecycle, and (2) an internal admin-only query API used by Support/Compliance to retrieve the audit timeline. Customer-visible release notes remain generated elsewhere; this is the compliance/support trace.

Scope:
- Emit audit events on: model_version.created, model_version.metadata_updated, model_versi...

title: Hotfix: prevent rollout UI from rendering internal-only model release notes for non-admin roles
preview: Context: During the rollouts UI dogfood of the new model change history / release notes surfaces, we found a case where internal-only note fields (e.g. `internal_summary`, `internal_risk_assessment`, and links to internal runbooks) could appear in the Console for standard (non-admin) roles. This is not expected and ...
text:
Context: During the rollouts UI dogfood of the new model change history / release notes surfaces, we found a case where internal-only note fields (e.g. `internal_summary`, `internal_risk_assessment`, and links to internal runbooks) could appear in the Console for standard (non-admin) roles. This is not expected and violates the public/internal separation we agreed on in ENG-9825.

Root cause: The Rollouts `ReleaseNotesCard` component was rendering the `details` payload verbatim when present. Under certain navigation paths (rollout timeline -> "Change details" -> "Release notes"), the UI was using an internal-capable response type and the renderer did not gate the internal sections on RBAC. While the backend should also enforce this (and does for the public feed), the UI should not trust the presence of fields.

Fix (hotfix / defense-in-depth): (1) Gate all internal-only sections in th...
